claim: KittenTTS is a 15M parameter TTS model in 25MB (int8 quantized), runs on CPU, and can be deployed in browsers or on edge devices
actionable: Use KittenTTS for client-side TTS in web apps or mobile; no GPU required and minimal bandwidth footprint

claim: KittenTTS quality degrades from 80M to 15M parameters but remains intelligible; 8-bit quantization adds artifacts but maintains voice consistency
actionable: For edge TTS, balance quality vs size; the 15M model works for many use cases; quantization helps but reduces quality

claim: KittenTTS struggles with punctuation; it doesn't reliably pause at sentence boundaries, tending to continue without breaks
actionable: Preprocess text for KittenTTS: add explicit pause markers if you need natural speech pacing

claim: KittenTTS uses Apache 2 license and is OSS, enabling browser-based and mobile deployment without licensing friction
actionable: Choose KittenTTS for open-source TTS needs where permissive licensing and small size are critical
